final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original, non-infringing traditional Chinese gongbi heavy-color painting depicting the "Hundred Birds Paying Homage to the Phoenix" (百鸟朝凤图).

Style/medium: Chinese gongbi (meticulous brush) heavy-color painting, traditional court painting style, fine ink outlines with layered mineral pigments, gold powder accents on plumage details, Song dynasty bird-and-flower painting tradition
Composition: panoramic scroll format, symmetrical layout with central phoenix pair as focal point, layered depth with foreground birds, midground phoenixes, and background mountains
Subject: male phoenix with vermillion red, golden yellow, and emerald green plumage; female phoenix with pink-purple, silver-white, and azure blue feathers; both soaring with wings spread in mutual response; surrounded by dozens of exotic birds including peacocks with fanned tails, dancing cranes, foraging golden pheasants, playful parrots, each with distinct poses
Scene/backdrop: ethereal immortal realm with steaming clouds and rosy dawn mist, towering mountains, ancient pine trees and verdant cypresses, exotic flowers and rare plants scattered throughout
Lighting/mood: soft diffused celestial light filtering through clouds, auspicious and majestic atmosphere suggesting prosperity and good fortune
Color palette: rich saturated mineral pigments with vermillion and gold as anchors, layered greens and blues for landscape, metallic gold for feather highlights, harmonious balance of warm and cool tones
Materials/textures: fine flowing ink lines defining forms, dense opaque color application, visible gold leaf or gold powder on plumage details, silk-like surface texture
Constraints: must reflect traditional Chinese painting techniques; phoenix pair must be clearly distinguished by color schemes; all bird species must be identifiable and distinct
Avoid: Western perspective, photographic realism, anime or cartoon styling, modern digital gradients, watermark, 8K stuffer terms
